turns.content:
You are subagent gsd-executor. Execute the fix for Phase 23.1 — Session-Tracker race condition. Read SPEC.md first for full requirements.

## Phase Context

Phase 23.1 SPEC at: `.planning/phases/23.1-session-tracker-sdk-investigation/23.1-SPEC.md`
Investigation report: `.planning/phases/23.1-session-tracker-sdk-investigation/23.1-SESSION-TRACKER-INVESTIGATION-2026-05-24.md`

## Root Cause (confirmed by investigation)

`plugin.ts:328` — `void sessionTracker.initialize()` is fire-and-forget async. Critical dependencies (`eventCapture`, `pendingRegistry`, `childWriter`, `hierarchyIndex`) are assigned via `Object.assign(this, deps)` INSIDE the async initialize method. Any `onChildSessionCreated` callback from delegate-task dispatch that fires before `Object.assign` completes hits `handleSessionEvent()` guard at `index.ts:140` — short-circuits, silently drops the event. This is why U4/U5 failed 4 rounds of live UAT.

Read the investigation report for full file:line references.

## 3 Requirements to Implement

### REQ-01 (PRIMARY): Sync dependency construction before delegation wiring
- Read `src/features/session-tracker/initialization.ts:85-192` — `constructDependencies()` sync factory
- Read `src/features/session-tracker/index.ts:456-533` — `initialize()` method
- Move sync `constructDependencies()` call + `Object.assign(this, deps)` to happen BEFORE `setupDelegationModules()` in plugin.ts, OR expose a sync init method that constructs deps immediately
- Ensure `eventCapture`, `pendingRegistry`, `childWriter`, `hierarchyIndex` are available before any `onChildSessionCreated` callback can fire
- Acceptance: Calling `onChildSessionCreated(sessionID, parentID)` immediately after `new SessionTracker()` writes child `.json` instead of dropping

### REQ-02 (DEFENSIVE): Add warning log for skipped events
- Read `src/features/session-tracker/index.ts:134-182` — `handleSessionEvent()`
- At line ~140 where `if (this.eventCapture)` guards, add `client.app?.log` warning with `[Harness] Session tracker: handleSessionEvent skipped — deps not initialized (sessionID: ..., eventType: ...)`
- This prevents future silent debugging of similar regressions

### REQ-03 (CLEANUP): Remove premature cleanup chain
- Read `src/plugin.ts:328-339` — sessionTracker.initialize() call
- Remove `.then(() => sessionTracker.cleanup())` — keep retry queue alive for plugin lifetime
- Change from: `void sessionTracker.initialize().then(() => sessionTracker.cleanup())`
- Change to: `void sessionTracker.initialize()`

## Files to Modify

| File | REQ | Change |
|------|-----|--------|
| `src/features/session-tracker/index.ts` | REQ-01, REQ-02 | Expose sync init for critical deps, add defensive logging |
| `src/features/session-tracker/initialization.ts` | REQ-01 | Ensure constructDependencies() can be called sync |
| `src/plugin.ts` | REQ-01, REQ-03 | Reorder init before delegation wiring, remove cleanup chain |

## Execution Order

1. Read all source files first
2. Implement REQ-01 (primary fix — reorder + sync deps)
3. Implement REQ-02 (defensive logging)
4. Implement REQ-03 (cleanup chain removal)
5. Run `npm run typecheck`
6. Run `npx vitest run tests/features/session-tracker/`
7. Run `npm test` (full suite)

## Constraints

- Atomic commit per REQ or one combined commit
- `npm run typecheck` MUST pass — no regressions
- `[Harness]` prefix on new log messages
- No inline comments
- JSDoc on exports
- import type, verbatimModuleSyntax

## Output

Return: what changed (file:line), typecheck result, test results, commit hash
